_legend.description: Top 100 most discriminative exemplars across both corpus runs, ranked by composite margin. Composite margin = minimum discrimination margin across all four faces (27cell, act, site, resolution). A high composite margin means the clause is unambiguously in its cell from every angle simultaneously.

corpus_a_ud.description: Universal Dependencies treebanks — 41 languages, diverse registers (news, Wikipedia, legal, literary, web)

corpus_b_specialist.description: arXiv quantum physics + Greek/Taoist/Stoic philosophy + biblical wisdom literature — English only
corpus_b_specialist.note: Discriminative exemplar computation not yet run for this corpus. All exemplars below are from Corpus A. When Corpus B exemplar analysis is computed, this file should be regenerated to merge both.

fields.rank: Global rank by composite margin (1 = most discriminative across all faces)
fields.source: Which corpus this exemplar comes from (corpus_a_ud or corpus_b_specialist)
fields.sim_own: Cosine similarity to own cell centroid [0-1]
fields.margin_composite: Minimum margin across all four faces — the ranking key
fields.rank_in_cell: Rank within its specific 27-cell (1 = top exemplar for that cell)

summary.note: All 100 exemplars are from Corpus A (UD) because Corpus B exemplar analysis has not been computed yet.
